Paraprasophyllum anticum is a species of orchid, commonly known as a terrestrial orchid, native to Australia. The plant is believed to have potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. Bioactive constituents of Paraprasophyllum anticum likely include flavonoids and phenolic compounds, although specific compounds have not been identified. Traditional herbal preparations made with Paraprasophyllum anticum may include infusions, tinctures, and extracts, although commercial use is limited due to the plant's rarity and lack of research.
